title: Real-site enablement: opt-in real tier, selector discovery, UC1-3 real acceptance (D-002)

headline: T1 (tier plumbing) and T1b (channel=cdp attach plumbing + mock-tier tests) are both DONE, manager-gate-verified, and committed (3693388, f9c330e). The full default suite is GREEN (136 passed / 1 deselected / exit 0). The real-site legs T2 (discovery) and T3 (UC1-3 acceptance) are OPERATOR-GATED: the CDP endpoint 127.0.0.1:9222 is CLOSED (operator on a trip; signed-in Chromium not running), so no real worker can attach. CDP_UNREACHABLE. 0 of 30 real messages spent; budget fully intact. This headless manager closed out cleanly per the operator-coordination rule (do not idle for the operator).

telemetry.REWORK-CAUSE_detail: T1b was re-run from scratch this turn because the operator paused the mission for a trip mid-leg (commit bb50a35) and its pi worker was hard-killed ~19m in, leaving unfinished/unverified/uncommitted edits. Per the pause record's option (a) those suspect edits were reverted to the clean T1 baseline and T1b was re-dispatched fresh. Not spec-gap / env-drift / frozen-file / dependency-rot.

Reverted to the clean T1 baseline via `git stash push -u -- src/ tests/` (the global destructive-guard.sh hook BLOCKS git checkout/clean) -> src/+tests/ == HEAD; suspect edits preserved in stash@{0} (recoverable; superseded).

New errors present: CDPUnreachableError (errors.py:18), ChallengePresentError (errors.py:26). channel=cdp attach present: connect_over_cdp (driver.py:408), CDP_UNREACHABLE token (driver.py:410), always-new-tab branch (driver.py:437). Scope clean (only driver/errors/test_driver + new cdp test + empty-map fixture + report). Report last line T1b-STATUS: DONE, MESSAGES_USED: 0.
uv used the PROJECT .venv, not the shared agent venv: the `VIRTUAL_ENV ... does not match ... will be ignored` warning confirms correct isolation (charter: never touch the shared agent venv).
CDP preflight (the real-leg gate): 127.0.0.1:9222 = PORT_9222_CLOSED at manager start (16:02), after T1b (16:26), and at handoff (16:32). Consistently closed -> T2/T3 cannot start.
Safety honored: no real-site contact, no login automation, no profile/cookie/token reads, no account identifiers, NO stealth/evasion (T1b is mock + throwaway-local-browser only).

blocker.owner: operator (launch the browser) -> team-lead (resume a manager)
blocker.condition: CDP endpoint 127.0.0.1:9222 is CLOSED. Real-site legs T2/T3 attach to the operator's own signed-in Chromium over CDP (operator decision B2); they cannot start until that browser is running with the debug port open. The operator is on a trip; the window is not open.
blocker.why_agent_cannot_resolve: The manager must NOT launch the operator's browser and must NEVER automate login; the operator owns and opens the signed-in browser window. This is by-design coordination, not a fault.
blocker.exact_operator_action: During a run window, launch (yourself): chromium --profile-directory='Profile 1' --remote-debugging-port=9222   (the 'agent' profile, signed into chatgpt.com). Keep it running. Then tell the team lead to resume MISSION-006 so a fresh manager can re-preflight CDP and dispatch the real discovery worker.
blocker.challenge_caveat: If, once attached, chatgpt.com shows a Cloudflare/human-verification UI, the driver raises ChallengePresentError and the manager runs the challenge-pause protocol (read-only poll <=10min for YOU to clear it in the window; else PARTIAL). The tool NEVER interacts with the challenge and NEVER uses stealth/evasion.

recommended_next_tasks.detail: GATED on the operator's CDP browser being up. Before dispatch the manager MUST: re-preflight 127.0.0.1:9222 reachable; REVISE the T2 contract (orchestration/tasks/M-006/T2-discovery.md was written for launch_persistent_context) for CDP — connect_over_cdp, ALWAYS open a new tab, NEVER touch operator tabs, challenge-pause protocol, log EVERY message to the ledger BEFORE send. Resolve the 10 observe-runbook unknowns; write PROPOSED selectors + discovery.md; do NOT edit src/real.json (T2b does).

Install T2's verified selectors into src/ask_chatgpt/selector_maps/real.json + extend real_allowlist with discovered asset domains + full suite green + commit. NOTE: T1b already decoupled the fail-closed test from live real.json (empty-map fixture), so T2b's old decouple sub-task is subsumed.

recommended_next_tasks.detail: channel=cdp config path; UC1 continuity via conversation-URL pinning + model_settings; UC2 bundle round-trip; UC3 via CLI. Revise the T3 contract for CDP before dispatch. Document the channel=cdp knob as the supported real mechanism in the runbooks.

recommended_next_tasks.task: T4 best-of-N verification panel (N=3 + synthesis; non-producers)
recommended_next_tasks.detail: Folds in the deferred T1 + T1b independent verification: (a) tier purity + CDP mock-tests + tab-hygiene/new-tab/detach adversarial proof + test_driver decouple; (b) D-002-as-amended conformance (grep for stealth/AutomationControlled patterns and confirm ABSENT; challenge protocol honored; budgets/audit consistent; no account identifiers); (c) empirical findings + spec coverage (10 unknowns; UC1-3 evidence; D-001 revisit). Synthesis -> VERIFICATION.md + orchestration/reports/M-006/verify.md with VERDICT: PASS|FAIL. Run the 3 lenses in PARALLEL over ONE shared authoritative pytest output; do not have 3 workers re-run the heavy suite concurrently.

complexity_paradigm_signals: TOOLING GOTCHA for future managers in this repo: the global ~/.claude/hooks/destructive-guard.sh BLOCKS `git checkout -- ...` and `git clean ...` (it substring-matches the WHOLE command, INCLUDING comments — a forbidden phrase in a comment will block an otherwise-safe command). Use `git stash push -u -- <paths>` to revert a dirty subtree non-destructively (it also preserves the work as a recoverable safety net). Keep destructive git verbs out of command text AND comments.

The CDP-attach pivot (B2) is the correct simplest design that honors the charter anchor 'real proof is operator-run': the tool drives the operator's OWN human browser (passes Cloudflare legitimately) rather than an automated browser (Cloudflare-blocked) or stealth (forbidden). T1b proves the mechanics with zero real-site contact, so the only remaining dependency for real proof is the operator opening the window.
Headless-manager discipline held: foreground watches only (--wait-seconds 480, looped), worker run-dir recorded before watching, handoff written before turn end. No backgrounded watches.
resume_ready_state_pointer: orchestration/state/M-006-state.json (status IN_PROGRESS; T1+T1b DONE; T2/T3 OPERATOR-GATED on CDP; blocker B2-CDP; budget 0/30; manager_pid 6057; HEAD f9c330e). A fresh manager: re-read this handoff + state, sibling-pid + budget re-check from BOTH ledgers (still 0), re-preflight CDP, and proceed from T2 ONLY if 127.0.0.1:9222 is reachable (else stay PARTIAL). Do NOT re-run T1/T1b. stash@{0} is the superseded suspect T1b edits (safe to drop).
